title: fraxinus oxycarpa var. sogdiana
intro: Fraxinus oxycarpa var. sogdiana, commonly known as the Sogdian ash, is a variety of the Fraxinus oxycarpa tree species native to Central Asia. The Sogdian ash is a deciduous tree with medicinal properties that have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. Its bark, leaves, and roots are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting cardiovascular health. The Sogdian ash can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in folk remedies and traditional medicine preparations.
